examples.title: Command Line Arguments Example
examples.description: A C++ program that takes two command line arguments: a string and an integer. The program prints the string and the integer to the console.
examples.explanation: This program uses the argc and argv variables to access the command line arguments. It checks if the correct number of arguments is provided, and if not, it prints an error message and returns 1. Otherwise, it converts the second argument to an integer using the stoi function and prints both arguments to the console.

examples.title: getopt Example
examples.description: A C++ program that uses the getopt function to parse command line arguments. The program takes two options: -h and -v. The -h option prints a help message, and the -v option prints the version number of the program.
examples.explanation: This program uses the getopt function to parse the command line arguments. It uses a while loop to iterate over the options, and a switch statement to handle each option. If an invalid option is provided, it prints an error message and returns 1.
